rationale: The citing paper gives Sprott et al. (2009) a clear functional role in both theory and empirics. It explicitly presents BESC as a nearby construct, argues how brand schematicity is conceptually different (self-brand inclusion vs. general information-processing organization), and uses that distinction to position its contribution. The authors also administer the BESC scale in Study 4 and report the correlation with brand schematicity as evidence of discriminant validity, so the citation is more than background mention.
relation_description: The citing paper refines the conceptual boundary around individual brand-related traits by distinguishing its new brand schematicity construct from Sprott et al.'s BESC construct. It also uses the BESC measure empirically as a discriminant-validity benchmark, showing the two constructs are not strongly related in its data.
